attractions.name: Black Building (also known as The Black) Black Building Fargo, Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Black Building is an eight-story Art Moderne masterpiece in the heart of downtown Fargo, originally completed in 1931 as the state's tallest building. Once a bustling center of commerce anchored by Sears and the WDAY radio studios, it has been meticulously renovated to house luxury 'built-to-suit' condos, modern office spaces, and ground-floor retail. The structure is celebrated for its Indiana limestone facade and is a designated historic resource on the National Register of Historic Places. Black Building Fargo, Wikipedia)

attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Offices/General Building); Retail hours vary by establishment LoopNet, Black Building Fargo
attractions.admission: Free Black Building Fargo

[Wikipedia)', "Features a striking Art Moderne exterior clad in Indiana limestone, departing from the area's typical brick. Wikipedia)", 'Historically served as the studio for WDAY radio, where legendary singer Peggy Lee began her career. Black Building Fargo', 'Preserved interior details include original terrazzo flooring, marble baseboards, and ornate elevator doors. Black Building Fargo', 'Now features luxury sky-high condominiums on its upper floors with expansive city views. Black Building Fargo', 'The building is connected to the downtown skyway system, providing easy indoor access to nearby parking and businesses. LoopNet']
attractions.tips: Visit the ground floor to explore local retail shops and admire the restored historical architecture. Look for the iconic 'Black' sign on the exterior, which was reinstalled in 2019 to honor the building's original 1930s design. Black Building Fargo

attractions.name: Bonanzaville, USA
attractions.description: Bonanzaville, USA is a 12-acre history museum complex and pioneer village dedicated to preserving the heritage of the Red River Valley. Operated by the Cass County Historical Society, it features 40 historic buildings moved from across the region, including a church, schoolhouse, and specialized museums for cars, aircraft, and law enforcement. The name pays tribute to the large-scale "bonanza farms" that were once prevalent in North Dakota agriculture during the late 19th century (Bonanzaville Official Website, Wikipedia).

More than just a mall, it serves as a vibrant community center showcasing the largest collection of local and regional art in the state and hosting numerous community events. (West Acres)
attractions.region: West Fargo / South Fargo area near I-29 and I-94 (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Store Hours: Mon-Sat: 10am-8pm, Sun: 10am-6pm; Walking Hours: Mon-Sat: 6:30am, Sun: 8:00am (West Acres)
attractions.admission: Free (West Acres)
highlights: "Home to over 100 local, regional, and national retailers, including major anchors like Macy's and JCPenney ([West Acres)", 'The Roger Maris Museum, dedicated to the legendary baseball player (Wikipedia)', 'The Regional Showcase program, featuring work from more than 100 local and regional artists (West Acres)', 'The District, a collection of unique indoor and outdoor dining destinations (Wikipedia)', 'Guest Services offering amenities like mall cards, stroller rentals, and gift wrapping (West Acres)', 'Community events such as The Market @ West Acres and Summer Story Squad (West Acres)']
attractions.tips: Take advantage of free amenities like wheelchairs and electric carts at Guest Services, or enjoy the walking hours starting as early as 6:30 am. Visit during the summer for local markets and community events. (West Acres) (West Acres)
attractions.nearby: Roger Maris Museum, Red River Zoo, Fargo-Moorhead Visitors Center, Thunder Road Amusement Park, Plains Art Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Würst Bier Hall Downtown Fargo
attractions.description: Nestled in the heart of downtown Fargo, Würst Bier Hall offers a vibrant, German-inspired dining and drinking experience with a lively communal atmosphere. The menu features an extensive selection of authentic German favorites, including gourmet sausages and traditional dishes like jäger schnitzel and currywurst. Würst Bier Hall Official Site
attractions.hours: Monday: 4:00 pm – 9:00 pm; Tuesday: 4:00 pm – 9:00 pm; Wednesday: 11:00 am – 9:00 pm; Thursday: 11:00 am – 9:00 pm; Friday: 11:00 am – 12:00 am; Saturday: 11:00 am – 12:00 am; Sunday: 11:00 am – 9:00 pm. Würst Bier Hall Official Site
attractions.admission: Free admission; prices for food and drink vary. Würst Bier Hall Official Site
highlights: 'Over 40 beers on tap, including local, regional, and seasonal brews [Official Site', 'Diverse selection of gourmet sausages including exotic options like elk, pheasant, and rattlesnake Official Site', 'The famous "Das Boot" communal beer drinking experience Official Site', 'Authentic German entrées such as jäger schnitzel, currywurst, and house-made spaetzle Official Site', 'Dog-friendly outdoor patio for four-legged friends Official Site', 'Separate vegan menu featuring plant-based sausages and snacks HappyCow']
attractions.tips: Minors under 21 are allowed daily until 9:00 p.m. when accompanied by an adult. For a unique experience, ask for the "Das Boot" or try their famous bacon flight. A dedicated vegan menu is available upon request at the Downtown location. Würst Bier Hall Official Site, HappyCow
attractions.nearby: Plains Art Museum, Fargo Theatre, Island Park, Broadway Square, Drekker Brewing Company. Visit Fargo-Moorhead

attractions.name: Fargo-Moorhead Community Theatre (Northern Prairie Performing Arts)
attractions.description: Founded in 1946, the Fargo-Moorhead Community Theatre (FMCT) is the oldest and largest community arts organization in the Red River Valley. It provides a wide range of theatrical and educational opportunities, producing a year-round schedule of plays and musicals in an intimate 344-seat thrust theatre (FMCT, North Dakota Tourism).
attractions.address: 202 1st Avenue North, Heritage Hall (inside Hjemkomst Center)
attractions.hours: Box Office (Phone/In-Person): Tuesday-Friday, 9:00 am - 5:00 pm. Performances: Typically Thursday-Saturday at 7:30 pm and Sunday at 5:30 pm. Lobby opens one hour prior to showtime (FMCT).
attractions.admission: Musicals: Adult $28, Senior (65+) $20, Student $15 (Arts People). Plays: Adult $25, Senior $18, Student $13 (Minnesota Accessible Activities Calendar). Discounted Flex Passes and $50 annual memberships are also available (FMCT).
highlights: 'Longest-running community arts organization in the region, established in 1946 ([FMCT).', 'Located within the iconic Hjemkomst Center in Heritage Hall (Explore Minnesota).', 'Intimate 344-seat thrust theatre design for an engaging audience experience (Explore Minnesota).', 'Comprehensive educational programs including youth camps and the Silver Follies for seniors (FMCT).', 'Commitment to accessibility with sensory-friendly and audio-described performances (FMCT).', 'Features an on-site full bar and concessions available before performances (FMCT).']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to enjoy concessions and the lobby bar which open 30 minutes before performances. The typical dress code is "office casual." Free parking is available in the south and east lots of the Hjemkomst Center (FMCT).
attractions.nearby: Hjemkomst Center (Hjemkomst Viking Ship & Hopperstad Stave Church), Viking Ship Park, Historical and Cultural Society of Clay County, and Rourke Art Gallery + Museum (Concordia College, Explore Minnesota).

attractions.name: Heritage Hjemkomst Interpretive Center (Hjemkomst Center)
attractions.description: The Heritage Hjemkomst Interpretive Center is a cultural and history museum that serves as the permanent home for the Hjemkomst Viking Ship and a full-scale replica of the Hopperstad Stave Church. Operated by the Historical & Cultural Society of Clay County, the center also houses the Clay County museum and archives, featuring exhibits on regional history and rotating gallery installations. City of Moorhead, HCSCC
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:00am - 5:00pm; Tuesday Evening (Free): 5:00pm - 8:00pm; Sunday: 12:00pm - 5:00pm. Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults (18-64): $12, Seniors (65+): $11, Youth (5-17): $8, Children (under 5): Free. Admission is free for everyone on Tuesday evenings from 5:00pm to 8:00pm. Official Website
highlights: ['The Hjemkomst Viking Ship, a hand-built replica that sailed to Norway in 1982.', 'A full-scale replica of the 12th-century Hopperstad Stave Church from Vik, Norway.', 'The Clay County Museum & Archives featuring local history artifacts.', 'A 12-minute documentary film chronicling the construction and voyage of the Hjemkomst ship.', 'Rotating art and history galleries featuring regional and traveling exhibits.', 'Outdoor grounds featuring the Heritage Garden and walking paths near the Red River.']
attractions.tips: Arrive on the top of the hour for guided tours of the Stave Church (available seasonally from April to December). It is recommended to watch the introductory film in the theater before viewing the Viking ship to fully appreciate its history and voyage. HCSCC Social Story
attractions.nearby: Rourke Art Museum, Comstock House, Bergquist Cabin, Fargo Theatre, Plains Art Museum, and the Red River Market. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Northern Lights Casino Hotel & Event Center
attractions.description: Northern Lights Casino Hotel & Event Center is a premier gaming destination in Northern Minnesota, owned and operated by the Leech Lake Band of Ojibwe (Leech Lake Tourism). The facility offers a vibrant gaming floor with over 900 slot machines, blackjack, and a dedicated poker room, complemented by diverse dining options and a full-service hotel (Northern Lights Casino). It serves as a central hub for live concerts, community events, and outdoor-themed relaxation in the scenic Leech Lake area (Northern Lights Casino).
attractions.hours: Casino: Open 24/7; North Star Restaurant: Breakfast 8 AM – 11 AM, Lunch 11 AM – 2 PM, Buffets 4 PM – 9 PM; River of Stars Cabaret: 10 AM – 1 AM (Northern Lights Casino) (Northern Lights Casino).
attractions.days_open: Daily (365 days a year) (Northern Lights Casino)
attractions.admission: Free for casino entry; ticket prices for events and concerts vary (Northern Lights Casino).
highlights: 'Over 900 slot machines featuring classic and the latest video slot titles ([Leech Lake Tourism).', 'Daily table games including Blackjack and Poker tournaments at the dedicated Poker Room (Northern Lights Casino).', 'Multiple dining venues such as the North Star Restaurant, Little Star Snack Bar, and Dancing Fire (Northern Lights Casino).', 'The River of Stars Cabaret, offering live music, weekly events like Bar Bingo, and daily drink specials (Northern Lights Casino).', 'On-site hotel with diverse accommodations including standard rooms and luxury jacuzzi suites (Northern Lights Casino).', 'An expansive event center hosting major concerts and mixed martial arts events (Northern Lights Casino).']
attractions.tips: Join the Players Club for rewards and special promotions, and check the event calendar for weekly highlights like Bar Bingo on Wednesdays (Northern Lights Casino). Guests must be at least 18 years old to register for a hotel room (Northern Lights Casino).
attractions.nearby: Leech Lake, Tianna Country Club, Shingobee Recreation Area, and the city of Walker (Expedia).

attractions.name: Wild Rice Bar & Grill
attractions.description: The Wild Rice Bar & Grill is a long-standing local establishment located in the unincorporated community of Wild Rice, North Dakota, just south of Fargo. It serves as a social hub for the area, offering a full menu of pub-style food, drinks, and hosting events like live shows and sports viewing. The venue is situated near the Wild Rice River and is a well-known landmark for residents and visitors traveling south of the Fargo-Moorhead metropolitan area.
attractions.hours: Sunday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Monday–Thursday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM; Friday–Saturday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM
attractions.admission: Free (Standard bar/restaurant entry)
highlights: ['Full food and drink menu with daily specials', 'Hosts live entertainment and community events', 'Outdoor seating area available during warmer months', 'Proximity to the Wild Rice River and local fishing spots', 'Casual, family-friendly atmosphere during daytime hours']
attractions.tips: Check their social media or website for specific event schedules, such as live music or themed nights. It is a popular spot for locals after visiting nearby river parks or fishing.
attractions.nearby: Wild Rice River, Heritage Hills Nature Park, Forest River Park, Red River of the North, City of Horace

attractions.name: Minn-Dak Farmers Cooperative
attractions.description: Minn-Dak Farmers Cooperative (MDFC) is a prominent sugarbeet processing cooperative owned by approximately 430 shareholders and over 100 growers. Formed in 1972 and producing its first crop in 1974, it processes sugarbeets into high-quality sugar, molasses, and co-products in the heart of the Red River Valley. MDFC About
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (Standard business office hours; factory operations run 24/7) LinkedIn
attractions.days_open: Monday-Friday (Main Office); Plant operates year-round with seasonal harvest peaks LinkedIn
attractions.admission: Free (Facility is a private processing plant; visitor access requires check-in at Security Office or Main Office Receptionist) MDFC Safety Policy
highlights: 'First sugarbeet cooperative established in the United States, formed in 1972 [MDFC About', 'Operates a 1,200-acre processing facility in Richland County, North Dakota MDFC About', 'Owned by a dedicated group of approximately 500 shareholders and growers MDFC About', 'Produces high-quality sugar and co-products for global distribution MDFC Home', 'Significant regional employer with over 400 year-round and hundreds of seasonal workers MDFC About', "Celebrated 50 years of 'Sweetening Life in the Valley' in 2022 MDFC About"]
attractions.tips: All visitors must sign in at the Security Office or Main Office; a 15 MPH speed limit is strictly enforced on the property. ID scanning is required for visitor badges, and safety rules must be followed at all times while on the processing grounds. MDFC Safety Policy

attractions.name: Bronze Buffalo Bar at The Lodge at Bronze Buffalo Ranch
attractions.description: The Bronze Buffalo Bar is a refined yet welcoming Western-style lounge located within the Lodge at Bronze Buffalo Ranch. It features elevated comfort fare, handcrafted cocktails, and panoramic views of the Big Hole Mountain Range and the resort's championship golf course. The venue blends the warmth of the West with upscale amenities, providing a place to relax and connect.
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Saturday: 4:00 PM - 9:00 PM (Après Ski/Happy Hour: 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM); Closed Sunday and Monday
attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant/Bar)
highlights: ['Signature cocktails and curated spirits', 'Panoramic mountain and golf course views', 'Western-themed upscale lounge atmosphere', 'Part of the Bronze Buffalo Sporting Club at Teton Springs', 'Gourmet seasonal small plates and appetizers', 'Indoor and patio seating with custom firepits']
attractions.tips: Reservations are recommended for dinner; check seasonal closure dates (mid-April to mid-May) before visiting; the patio is home to 'Cowboy Cauldrons' for seasonal outdoor dining experiences.
attractions.nearby: Grand Teton National Park, Teton Reserve Golf Course, Grand Teton Brewing Company, Jackson Hole, WY

attractions.name: Buffalo River State Park
attractions.description: Buffalo River State Park conserves one of Minnesota's finest and largest remnant prairies, bisected by the wooded banks of the Buffalo River. The landscape features over 250 species of wildflowers and grasses, providing a home to diverse wildlife and offering opportunities for hiking, picnicking, and camping (Minnesota DNR, Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Park hours: Daily 8:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Ranger station (Spring 2026): Monday - Sunday 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Minnesota DNR)
attractions.admission: Park permits: $7 daily, $35 annual, $26 second vehicle, $12 handicapped (Explore Minnesota)
highlights: "One of Minnesota's largest and highest-quality remnant prairies ([Wikipedia))", 'Extensive hiking trails along the Buffalo River and through the prairie (Minnesota DNR)', 'Native prairie landscape featuring over 250 species of wildflowers and grasses (Explore Minnesota)', 'Diverse wildlife viewing, including bobolinks, prairie chickens, and over 40 species of mammals (Fargo Mom)', 'Riverside picnicking and 44 drive-in campsites (Minnesota DNR)']
attractions.tips: The swimming pond is permanently closed. Purchase firewood at the office or via Yodel to prevent spreading tree-killing insects. Best wildflower viewing is in spring and fall. Camping reservations should be made online (Minnesota DNR, Fargo Mom).
attractions.nearby: Bluestem Prairie Scientific and Natural Area (SNA), MSUM Regional Science Center, City of Hawley, Moorhead Antique Mall (Explore Minnesota)

attractions.name: Flaten Art Museum (located at St. Olaf College)
attractions.description: The Flaten Art Museum is a premier teaching museum at St. Olaf College that collects, interprets, and exhibits regional, national, and international art. It serves as a vital educational resource, engaging students and the community in creative and critical thinking through its diverse permanent collection and rotating contemporary exhibitions. St. Olaf College, Wikipedia

attractions.description: Frontier Village is a recreated pioneer town featuring original buildings from across North Dakota, including a schoolhouse, church, and jail. Visitors can explore informative historical displays, visit the Louis L’Amour writer’s shack, and see the World’s Largest Buffalo monument nearby Discover Jamestown, North Dakota Tourism

attractions.name: International Peace Garden
attractions.description: The International Peace Garden is a 2,400-acre botanical garden and recreation area situated on the international border between North Dakota and Manitoba. Founded in 1932, it serves as a living monument to peace and cooperation between nations, featuring formal gardens, a world-class cacti conservatory, and scenic trails (International Peace Garden, Tripadvisor).
attractions.region: Turtle Mountains, Rolette County (ND) / Municipality of Boissevain – Morton (MB)
attractions.hours: Gardens: 8 am – 8 pm; Don Vitko Cacti & Succulents & The Conservatory: 10 am – 6 pm; Gift Shop: 10 am – 6 pm; Admin Office: Mon-Fri 8 am – 4:30 pm; Gate House: Open Daily (International Peace Garden)
attractions.admission: Day Pass: $12 per person (Ages 6+), Free for children 5 and younger; Annual Household Membership: $50; Lifetime Membership: $500; School Bus: $8.00 per student; Tour Bus: $225; Transport/Shuttle Van: $100 one-time fee or $150 annual entry fee (International Peace Garden)
highlights: 'Formal Gardens featuring over 80,000 annuals and perennials ([International Peace Garden)', 'Don Vitko Cacti & Succulents Collection with over 5,000 species (International Peace Garden)', '9/11 Memorial featuring steel girders from the World Trade Center (International Peace Garden)', 'Sunken Garden with octagonal ponds and reflecting pools (International Peace Garden)', 'The Floral Clock, a 18-foot wide display (International Peace Garden)', 'Peace Chapel with walls inscribed with inspirational quotes (International Peace Garden)']
attractions.tips: Visitors must go through customs upon leaving the garden, so it is recommended to have a passport or birth certificate ready. Plan for at least 3-4 hours to see the main attractions. You can stand in both the U.S. and Canada simultaneously at the border marker (Tripadvisor, International Peace Garden).
attractions.nearby: North American Game Warden Museum, William Lake Provincial Park, Turtle Mountain Provincial Park, Lake Storman (Tripadvisor, International Peace Garden)

attractions.name: Sheyenne National Grassland
attractions.description: Sheyenne National Grassland is the only National Grassland in the tallgrass prairie region of the U.S., spanning over 70,000 acres of public land in southeastern North Dakota. It features a unique landscape of sandy soils, rolling sand dunes, and oak savannas, providing a vital habitat for the largest population of greater prairie chickens in the state.
attractions.address: 1601 Main Street, Lisbon, ND 58054 (Sheyenne Ranger District Office)
attractions.region: Southeastern North Dakota (Ransom and Richland Counties)
attractions.hours: Grassland: Open 24 hours daily; Sheyenne Ranger District Office: Monday-Friday 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Closed on federal holidays) (US Forest Service)
attractions.admission: Free for day use and dispersed camping; $10.00 per night for campsites at Jorgen's Hollow and Hankinson Hills campgrounds.
highlights: 'Only National Grassland in the tallgrass prairie region of the U.S. ([Wikipedia)', 'Home to the largest population of greater prairie chickens in North Dakota (US Forest Service)', '31-mile segment of the North Country National Scenic Trail for hiking and biking (Wikipedia)', 'Diverse wildlife including Dakota skipper butterflies and western prairie fringed orchids (US Forest Service)', 'Scenic landscapes ranging from deltaic plains to choppy sand dunes (US Forest Service)', 'Opportunities for primitive camping, horseback riding, and birdwatching (The Dyrt)']
attractions.tips: Visit in early spring to view greater prairie chickens on their booming grounds. Dispersed camping is allowed on public land for up to 14 days, but ensure you are not on private property. Bring your own water or be prepared to treat water from hand pumps. (US Forest Service, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: McLeod Museum Complex, Owego Pioneer Cemetery, Sheyenne River, Iron Springs Creek, Horseshoe Hills, Old Fire Lookout Tower (Outdooractive)
